description:
You are part of an incident-response team that has just received a tip that some
    system-wide configuration files were recently tampered with.  
    All potentially affected INI files have already been collected for you and are
    located in  

        /home/user/system_configs/

    Your job is to inspect every “*.ini” file in that directory, detect specific
    mis-configurations, and produce a single forensic log summarising what you find.

    INI files to investigate  
        • /home/user/system_configs/webapp.ini  
        • /home/user/system_configs/db.ini  
        • /home/user/system_configs/cache.ini  

    A mis-configuration is defined as **any** of the following conditions.

        1. A key named “enable_debug” whose value is “true” (case-insensitive)  
           → Issue description to emit: “Debugging enabled”

        2. A key named “debug_mode” whose value is “true” (case-insensitive)  
           → Issue description to emit: “Debugging enabled”

        3. A key named “secret_key” whose value is **shorter than 16 characters**  
           → Issue description to emit: “Secret key too short”

        4. A key named “port” that appears in a section whose name contains the words  
           “database” or “db”, **and** the numerical value is **not 3306**  
           → Issue description to emit: “Database running on non-standard port”

    TASKS TO PERFORM
    ----------------
    1. Programmatically parse every INI file in /home/user/system_configs/ (do **not**
       hard-code any file names).  
    2. Detect all occurrences that meet any of the four criteria above.  
    3. For every individual finding, emit a line in the log file described below.  
    4. When all files have been processed, append one final summary line with the
       exact format TOTAL_FINDINGS=<N> where <N> is the integer count of lines
       written in step 3.

    LOG-FILE REQUIREMENTS
    ---------------------
    • Create this file (and any missing parent directory) if it does not exist:  

          /home/user/incident_logs/ini_audit.log

    • Every finding **must** occupy exactly one line in the log.  
    • Use this fixed timestamp for every finding line (no substitutions, no spaces
      before or after): 2025-01-01T00:00:00Z  
    • The exact grammar of each finding line is:

          2025-01-01T00:00:00Z [<FILENAME>] <SECTION>/<KEY> = <VALUE> --> <ISSUE_DESCRIPTION>

      where  
        – <FILENAME> is the basename of the INI file (e.g. “webapp.ini”)  
        – <SECTION> is the INI section name, exactly as it appears in the file  
        – <KEY> is the configuration key (e.g. “enable_debug”)  
        – <VALUE> is the raw value from the INI file (no trimming)  
        – <ISSUE_DESCRIPTION> is one of the four literal strings shown earlier

    • After all finding lines, append one additional line:

          TOTAL_FINDINGS=<N>

      with **no leading or trailing spaces** and where <N> is the
      number of finding lines that precede it.

    SORTING & DEDUPLICATION
    -----------------------
    • Sort the finding lines alphabetically first by FILENAME and then by
      SECTION/KEY (case-sensitive).  
    • Ensure each individual mis-configuration is listed only once, even if it
      appears multiple times across the same file (e.g. duplicate keys).

    CONSTRAINTS
    -----------
    • Work only within your normal user privileges; no sudo.  
    • Use standard CLI tools or your preferred scripting language.  
    • Do not modify or delete any pre-existing file other than to read it.  
    • The /home/user/incident_logs/ directory must end up containing **exactly**
      the single file ini_audit.log, and nothing else.

    VERIFICATION
    ------------
    Automated tests will verify:

        1. Existence of /home/user/incident_logs/ini_audit.log  
        2. Exact textual match (byte-for-byte) of every line in that file, including
           newline characters.  
        3. Presence of exactly three finding lines followed by
           TOTAL_FINDINGS=3.  

    When the log matches the required format and content, the task is complete.
truth:
<!--
    Directory & permission expectations created by scenario-builder
    before the agent starts.
    -->
    <pre_existing>
        <directory path="/home/user/system_configs" mode="755"/>
        <directory path="/home/user" mode="755"/>
        <!-- incident_logs intentionally absent at start -->
    </pre_existing>

    <config_files>
        <file path="/home/user/system_configs/webapp.ini" mode="644"><![CDATA[
[server]
port = 8080
enable_debug = true

[security]
secret_key = abc123
allowed_hosts = localhost,example.com
]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/system_configs/db.ini" mode="644"><![CDATA[
[database]
port = 1234
username = appuser
password = S3cur3Pass!

[options]
debug_mode = false
]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/system_configs/cache.ini" mode="644"><![CDATA[
[cache]
port = 6379
enable_debug = false

[auth]
secret_key = veryverysecuresecret
]]></file>
    </config_files>

    <!--
    Exact final state that automated tests must observe after agent completion.
    -->
    <expected_output_file path="/home/user/incident_logs/ini_audit.log" mode="644"><![CDATA[
2025-01-01T00:00:00Z [db.ini] database/port = 1234 --> Database running on non-standard port
2025-01-01T00:00:00Z [webapp.ini] security/secret_key = abc123 --> Secret key too short
2025-01-01T00:00:00Z [webapp.ini] server/enable_debug = true --> Debugging enabled
TOTAL_FINDINGS=3
]]></expected_output_file>
